request: Fix destructive_cmd_guard/writesConsentPath false-positive: a `git commit` whose message payload (-m / -F / heredoc) merely contains a consent basename (commit_consent/push_consent/*_grant) plus a write-verb word (tee/cp/mv/...) is wrongly blocked, because the guard scans the whole command string including the message. Carve out git-commit message payloads from consent-path scanning while still blocking real Bash writes to consent paths, including in compound commands (e.g. `git commit -m x; tee .../commit_consent` must still be caught).
